title: lithocarpus pusillus
intro: Lithocarpus pusillus, also known as the small-footed or dwarf tanbark oak, is a small evergreen tree native to China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Its health benefits include relieving fever, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The herbal preparations of Lithocarpus pusillus typically involve drying and processing the leaves and bark into teas, infusions, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
